prestate_archive.reason: RV-1 checkpoint preserves the full pre-update queue in Git history; compact state restates active execution fields without treating the DSM-1 receipt as validated.

progress.note: BEM949-DSM-1 confirmed DONE via a genuine 2026-07-02 GitHub Actions run (run_id 28614313748), verified through two independent sources: the repository's own dispatch_lifecycle.jsonl (STATE_COMMITTED/COMPLETED/success) and an operator-supplied actual job log matching the same trace_id/task_id. The dsm1_finalize.py ts() bug at the time prevented this success from being converted into a receipt back then, causing the earlier misfile and confusion. That is now resolved. P7 preconditions are all satisfied.

tasks.resume_condition: GitHub Actions run-level outcome/log access or operator-supplied run URL/screenshot.

tasks.sha_correction_note: Prior recorded sha (bfb0a31f50271cb765c64c664b0fe1fa46b22e6f4, 41 chars) was malformed -- one extra character, same class of bug as the historical P0 commit-sha typo. Corrected to the actual 40-char git blob sha after direct file read by EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE during full repository audit.

history.root_cause: dsm1_finalize.py had an undefined-name (ts()) bug at the time of the 2026-07-02 successful run, preventing the success from being converted into a receipt; fixed under BEM950-T6.

tasks.description: Resolve RULE-004..010 in rule_enforcement_matrix.json with code_enforced, policy_only, or not_enforced plus evidence.
limitations: This classification does not constitute Broad Release PASS.
This classification does not validate the separate DSM-1 runtime receipt.
RULE-005..010 remain policy_only and NOT_ENFORCED at runtime.

tasks.next_step: GPT must prepare P7 audit package v3 reflecting the now-genuine DSM-1 status. Final PASS/FAIL verdict on P7 is issued only by EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE after direct repository verification, not by GPT self-report.

tasks.description: Two real bugs observed in the 2026-07-04 DSM-1 dispatch attempts: (1) 'git pull --rebase' fails with exit 128 when the working tree has unstaged changes from the finalizer run — needs --autostash or an explicit stash/reset before rebase. (2) Multiple concurrent autopilot attempts race on writing governance/roadmap/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, causing rejected pushes ('fetch first'). Needs either a retry-with-rebase-on-push loop or serialization (e.g. a lock file / concurrency group) so parallel runs don't collide.
acceptance_criteria: Fix all five bugs listed above, not just the one the operator's CI log surfaced.
Actually run `python3 -m py_compile` on all three files and paste the real terminal output (or exit code) in the next receipt -- do not restate a boolean claim without the underlying command output.
Re-verify dsm1_finalize.py and dispatch_executor.py by tracing through the success path by hand (or with a local test) since both have bugs that only manifest at runtime, not at compile time.
Original BEM950-T7 acceptance criteria (git rebase/race fixes, queue-writer concurrency group) still apply and have not yet been demonstrated live.
tasks.note:
GPT's T7 receipt (governance/proofs/BEM950_t7_autopilot_git_race_repair_receipt.json) explicitly claims python3_m_py_compile=true for all three runner files and syntax_defects_corrected_in_one_pass=true. This is FALSE, confirmed by (a) the operator's own CI log showing IndentationError at roadmap_autopilot.py line 89, and (b) EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE reading every file directly. Five distinct bugs found across three files, none matching the receipt's claim:
roadmap_autopilot.py (still does not compile):
  1. `if force_task_id and force_task_id != TASK_ID;` -- semicolon instead of colon. SAME BUG flagged in the prior T7 attempt, still not fixed.
  2. `if termal_blocked and attempts >= MAX_GENUINE_ATTEMPTS:` -- typo, should be `terminal_blocked` (defined a few lines above). NameError at runtime, separate from the compile failure.
  3. `task.update\n    {...}` (appears twice) -- missing call parentheses: `task.update` is a bare attribute reference, then an indented dict literal follows on the next line, which is exactly the IndentationError the operator's log shows.
dispatch_executor.py (compiles, but has a runtime bug):
  4. `if isinstace(run_id, int)` -- typo for `isinstance`. Compiles fine (py_compile only checks syntax) but raises NameError the first time a candidate run is matched.
dsm1_finalize.py (compiles, but has two runtime bugs):
  5a. `detail.get("termal_state")` -- typo for `terminal_state`; the success condition can never be true even on a genuine success, because it checks a field name that will never appear in real lifecycle results.
  5b. The receipt dict is assigned to a variable named `recipt` (missing the middle e), but every later reference uses `receipt` -- `write_json(RUNTIME_RECEIPT, receipt)` and both `print(json.dumps(receipt, ...))` calls reference an undefined name. This is a guaranteed NameError on every single invocation, blocked or passed.
GPT must not report a compile/test result without having actually run it against the exact committed content.

tasks.description: Direct contradiction found during full repository audit: governance/proofs/BEM949_p5_rule_enforcement_complete_receipt.json (created 2026-06-22) asserts runtime_execution_claim=true and all_target_rules_enforced=true for RULE-004 through RULE-010, with both positive and negative runtime validation evidence for all seven rules. governance/proofs/BEM949_rv2_rule_enforcement_receipt.json (created 2026-07-03, externally verified) reclassifies RULE-005 through RULE-010 as policy_only and explicitly NOT_ENFORCED at runtime, with RULE-004 code_enforced only in the narrow registry-schema scope. These cannot both be accurate for the same rules at different times without an explanation of what changed.
acceptance_criteria: Determine whether the P5 runtime-enforcement claim was accurate at the time (2026-06-22) — if so, identify what regressed RULE-005..010 to policy_only by 2026-07-03, and when/why.
If the P5 claim was inflated or scoped narrower than it states, document that honestly rather than silently reconciling it.
Do not resolve this by editing either historical receipt's content; add a new investigation receipt that states the finding plainly.
This must be resolved (or explicitly acknowledged as an open, unresolved discrepancy) before any Broad Release PASS is claimed for BEM-949.

open_blockers.summary: T7 receipt falsely claims python3 -m py_compile passed for all three runner files. It did not: roadmap_autopilot.py still fails to compile (same old semicolon bug, unfixed, plus two new bugs), and the other two files compile but contain undefined-name bugs that will crash at runtime (isinstace, termal_state, recipt/receipt mismatch). This is a false verification claim, not just an unfinished task.
P5 (2026-06-22) claims full runtime enforcement of RULE-004..010; RV-2 (2026-07-03) says RULE-005..010 are NOT_ENFORCED at runtime. Direct contradiction, unexplained.

next_action: GPT: fix all five confirmed bugs in roadmap_autopilot.py, dispatch_executor.py, and dsm1_finalize.py (listed in BEM950-T7's note). Do not claim a compile/test result without pasting real command output. P5/RV2 contradiction investigation and P7 v3 remain separately pending and are unaffected by this.

audit_note_2026_07_04.scope: Full re-verification of every task marked DONE/verified in the queue, performed independently of prior in-conversation verifications.
audit_note_2026_07_04.result_summary: 12 of 15 checked items matched exactly on direct file read. 1 malformed sha corrected (P3). 1 unresolved historical contradiction opened for investigation (P5 vs RV-2). 1 traceability gap noted (T4 has no receipt_sha in the queue). P6's sha256_content could not be independently reconstructed byte-exact by manual means; content is internally coherent but this receipt's hash claim is UNVERIFIED, not confirmed.
